reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: worker.js loads a REAL transformers.js zero-shot-classification pipeline (loadPipeline({task:'zero-shot-classification', model:'Xenova/nli-deberta-v3-xsmall', backend:'wasm', dtype:'q8'})) and calls pipe(text, labels, {hypothesis_template:'This example is {}.', multi_label}) on-device. Zero-shot is genuine NLI: each label becomes the hypothesis 'This example is {label}.' and the model's entailment probability is the label score — no fixed label set, labels invented at run time. Conformance calls-real-inference (source pattern) and loads-clean (console-clean) tested pass; io-shape-semantic manual-evidenced. No measured latency claimed — code-grounded retrospective backfill.
rubric.notes: Each label is a SEPARATE forward pass over (text, hypothesis), so cost scales with the number of labels — an inherent property of NLI-based zero-shot, disclosed via the per-label See-inside view.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = labels ranked by score. worker.js returns scored = out.labels.map((label,i)=>({label, score: out.scores[i]})) from the pipeline, PLUS nli[]: the raw 3-way [contradiction, neutral, entailment] logits + softmax per hypothesis, using pipe.model.config.id2label for the class names. io-shape-semantic conformance assertion covers a real run producing scored labels.
rubric.notes: multi_label toggle switches between softmax-over-labels (single-label) and independent sigmoid-style per-label entailment (multi-label) — the real pipeline semantics, exposed as a checkbox.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares Xenova/nli-deberta-v3-xsmall (linked HF card, 5 occurrences), the zero-shot-classification task, q8 dtype, WASM backend, ~70 MB and MIT license — matching models.json. declares-model-id/task/backend/quantisation conformance assertions tested pass.
rubric.notes: The hypothesis template 'This example is {}.' is disclosed in the See-inside copy so a reader understands how a label becomes a hypothesis. Inference is off the main thread.

rubric.evidence: The page uses the shared createModelLoader auto-init (modelId Xenova/nli-deberta-v3-xsmall, q8, ~70 MB, requiresWebGPU:false); a valid current cached copy auto-initialises with no Load click. shared-auto-init-loader conformance assertion tested pass.
rubric.notes: Only Download/Re-download/Update/Retry/Clear-cache surface per the shared loader — no silent large re-download.

rubric.evidence: runStatus is role=status aria-live; result and See-inside regions are aria-live. Inference errors are caught in the worker (post error) and surfaced on the page; the shared loader adds checking→initialising→ready + Retry. live-status-region and errors-on-the-page conformance assertions tested pass; honest-progress-retry-offline / runs-without-webgpu-fallback manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: WASM-only, no WebGPU needed — runs everywhere.

rubric.evidence: Real, labelled controls: the text <textarea> (id=text), the candidate-labels input (id=labels, comma-separated), the multi-label checkbox (id=multi), and the Run button (id=run). All keyboard-operable. controls-operable conformance assertion is manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: Inventing your own labels at run time is the whole point of zero-shot; the labels input is the primary control and it is a plain text field so any label set works.

rubric.evidence: No canned scores: the ranked labels come from the real pipeline and the See-inside 3-way NLI bars are the raw logits softmaxed in the worker from the same model instance. no-fake-output conformance assertion is manual-evidenced.

Run button and controls carry accessible names (buttons-have-names tested pass); status is role=status aria-live; result/See-inside regions aria-live; page lang=en; WCAG-AA design system light+dark. images-have-alt tested pass.
rubric.notes: The score bars are DOM elements with text percentages, so the ranking is available to assistive tech, not only visually.

rubric.evidence: responsive-desktop/responsive-mobile (no-horizontal-overflow) conformance assertions present for both classes; the layout uses the fluid design-system panels + flex-wrap so bars and the label row reflow. models.json support is needs-review on both classes (automated pre-scan flagged sub-24px tap targets), pending an agent matrix pass to confirm tap sizes and flip to ok.
rubric.notes: Recorded honestly as needs-review, not claimed ok.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs in the Web Worker so the UI stays responsive; the main thread only renders bars. inference-off-main-thread conformance assertion tested pass; inp-stays-low manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: Because each label is a separate NLI pass, a very long label list makes a run take proportionally longer — but the work is off-thread, so it does not block INP; the page could add incremental per-label progress for long lists (open item).

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move heavy work off main thread web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy work off the main thread; keep interactions responsive (INP).
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the pipeline call and the per-label raw-logit passes run in the worker; the main thread only paints scores and NLI bars.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread; inference-off-main-thread conformance assertion tested pass

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible form labels text input textarea
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Give every input a visible associated label; make controls keyboard-operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — text, candidate-labels and multi-label controls each have labels and are keyboard-operable; labels input is a plain text field so any label set is valid.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status role status aria-live
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce state via role=status aria-live; give controls accessible names.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — runStatus role=status aria-live; result + See-inside regions aria-live; Run button named.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: live-status-region + buttons-have-names conformance assertions tested pass

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow flex grid
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid flex/grid with wrapping and intrinsic sizing; avoid fixed widths.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — design-system panels + flex-wrap score bars and label row; full-width textarea.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive assertions present; support needs-review pending agent matrix pass

openQuestions: Should a long candidate-label list show incremental per-label progress (they run sequentially), so the user sees the ranking build up rather than waiting for the whole batch?
Is the DeBERTa-v3-xsmall (22M) q8 backbone strong enough for nuanced label sets, or should the page point users to the bart-zero-shot demo (larger, q4f16) when discrimination matters?

followUpGoals.goal: Add incremental per-label progress to the run so each label's entailment score appears as it is computed, improving perceived responsiveness for large label sets.
Run the mobile+desktop responsive matrix, confirm/raise tap targets, and flip models.json support to ok.

summary: Retrospective, code-grounded revision-1 critique for zero-shot-text (Xenova/nli-deberta-v3-xsmall, zero-shot-classification, q8, ~70 MB, MIT). Genuine NLI-based zero-shot: worker.js runs the transformers.js pipeline off the main thread with the hypothesis template 'This example is {}.', and reuses the same model instance to expose the raw 3-way contradiction/neutral/entailment logits per hypothesis in 'See inside'. Real controls (text, invent-your-own candidate labels, multi-label toggle), auto-init via createModelLoader, role=status aria-live states, no fake output. Verified against the immutable conformance suite (calls-real-inference, loads-clean, declares-*, inference-off-main-thread, shared-auto-init-loader, live-status-region, buttons-have-names tested pass; io-shape/parity/inp manual-evidenced). No fabricated numbers. Honest weaknesses: cost scales with label count (each label is a separate forward pass) and could show incremental progress; the tiny xsmall backbone is modest for nuanced labels; models.json support needs-review pending a tap-target matrix pass.
